title: prunus insititia subvar. desvauxii
intro: Prunus insititia subvar. desvauxii, also known as sloe, is a shrub or small tree that belongs to the Prunus genus, characterized by its dark purple or black fruits and thorny branches. The medicinal properties of Prunus insititia subvar. desvauxii include its high content of anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of consuming sloe fruits or using its extracts include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, sloe is often used to make jams, wines, and teas, and its bark and leaves can be used to make infusions for treating digestive issues and relieving pain.
